description.current: Audit app readiness across 8 phases: code quality, security, performance, reliability, observability, documentation, deployment. Produces READY/NOT READY/CONDITIONAL verdict with blocking issues.
issues: Slightly short (26 words) but comprehensive list
Clear 'when' context: before production deployment or major releases

issues.description: Phases 1-7 are enormous checklist sections (50+ lines combined) with minimal explanation of WHY each check matters or HOW to interpret results. Risk: users check boxes without understanding risk.
Phase 2 (Security) checklist lacks specificity. 'Input sanitization: Submit XSS payloads in forms' — what is a realistic XSS payload? How do you verify it's sanitized? Users must guess.
Phase 3 (Performance) references Lighthouse but doesn't explain what 'LCP under 2.5s' means in context or how to set up Lighthouse audit. External dependency not documented.
Phase 5 (Observability) asks to 'Review analytics/monitoring setup' without defining what minimum viable observability looks like. Vague acceptance criteria.
Severity Rules (lines 191-197) define blocking criteria (Security/Deployment FAIL = NOT READY) but don't explain rollback scenario: if you shipped a FAIL, what's the recovery?

strengths: 8-phase pipeline diagram (lines 26-36) shows orchestration and parallelization opportunity
Comprehensive checklist across 8 dimensions (code, security, perf, reliability, observability, docs, deployment)
Final report template (lines 157-187) is well-structured and includes blocking/non-blocking distinction
Severity Rules are unambiguous about blocking (Security, Deployment)
Related Skills section ties audit to feature validation workflow

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: No automated checklist runner. Script could execute Phase 1 checks (grep for TODO/FIXME, check for hardcoded secrets, scan dependencies) and produce structured report. Phases 2-7 require manual inspection but Phase 1 is mostly automatable.

top_fixes: Add context for each phase: 'Phase 1 catches common coding mistakes. Phase 2 validates security boundaries. Phase 3 ensures performance isn't degraded.' — Why matters, not just what to check.
For Phase 2 (Security), provide concrete XSS test strings: ``, `">`. Show expected sanitized output.
For Phase 3 (Performance), link to Lighthouse setup guide or bundle a script: `npm install -g lighthouse && lighthouse https://localhost:3000 --output=json > perf-report.json`
For Phase 5 (Observability), define MVP: 'At minimum: error tracking (Sentry or equivalent), 1 dashboard showing error rate and response time, health check endpoint'
Bundle script: audit-phase-1.sh that automates TODO/FIXME/secrets scan and produces structured report for manual review of phases 2-7

test_prompts.prompt: I'm auditing a Node.js API before first production deploy. It's currently at Phase 3 (Performance). The Lighthouse score is 85, but LCP is 3.2s (exceeds 2.5s target). Is this READY or CONDITIONAL?
test_prompts.expected_output: Should say: 'CONDITIONAL. Performance isn't blocked (Phase 3 failure doesn't block shipping per Severity Rules), but document the finding. Investigate why LCP is 3.2s (slow API, large bundle, unoptimized queries). Make it a known risk acceptance or schedule optimization post-launch.'

test_prompts.prompt: During Phase 2 (Security) audit, I notice JWT tokens in the URL params. Is this an automatic NOT READY?
test_prompts.expected_output: Should say: 'YES. Phase 2 Security finding: sensitive data in URLs. This is blocking per Severity Rules (any Phase 2 FAIL = NOT READY). Fix: remove JWT from URL, use Authorization header instead. Re-audit Phase 2 before deploying.'

test_prompts.prompt: My app passes all 8 phases. Documentation (Phase 6) is incomplete but we're under time pressure. Can we ship as CONDITIONAL?
test_prompts.expected_output: Should say: 'Yes, Phase 6 Documentation failure = CONDITIONAL (non-blocking). But: users will struggle to understand deployment, team onboarding will be hard, debugging in production will be slower. Schedule doc completion within 2 weeks post-launch. Document this as accepted risk.'
